scaled_oracle_cases.description: Test parsing of exchange files with unambiguous extensions (.tsv, .yaml) and ambiguous extensions (.txt) using content-based detection. Also verify that ambiguous content raises ParseError.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that the function correctly detects file format from content when extension is ambiguous (.txt or no extension) and raises ParseError for unsupported content.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: The function must read the first line of files with ambiguous extension and detect tab for TSV, colon for YAML; else raise ParseError.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed behavior (extension-only) would treat .txt as TSV regardless of content, thus failing to parse YAML content correctly and not raising ParseError for invalid content.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
import tempfile, os, sys
# Unambiguous TSV
with open('unambiguous.tsv', 'w') as f:
    f.write('compound\tlower\tupper\n')
    f.write('A\t-1\t10\n')
# Unambiguous YAML
with open('unambiguous.yaml', 'w') as f:
    f.write('X:-100:50:cytosol\n')
# Ambiguous .txt with TSV content
with open('ambiguous_tsv.txt', 'w') as f:
    f.write('compound\tlower\tupper\n')
    f.write('B\t0\t5\n')
# Ambiguous .txt with YAML content
with open('ambiguous_yaml.txt', 'w') as f:
    f.write('Y:0:200:mito\n')
# Ambiguous .txt with unsupported content
with open('ambiguous_fail.txt', 'w') as f:
    f.write('This is not valid\n')
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include at least one case where file extension is ambiguous (e.g., .txt or no extension) and content determines format.
Scaled executable cases must include at least one case where content is ambiguous and ParseError is raised.
